title: hieracium lachenalii subsp. acutatifrons
intro: Hieracium lachenalii subsp. acutatifrons, also known as hawkweed, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion-like genus Hieracium, native to the Mediterranean region. It is a perennial herb with leaves that are edible and have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of Hieracium lachenalii subsp. acutatifrons are used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
